# New-Alpha Discovery Study — `equity_sectors`

**Date:** 2026-06-05 · **Universe:** `config/instruments/equity_sectors.yaml` (104 large-caps, ~11 GICS sectors)
**Sample:** 6,644 daily bars, 2000-01-03 → 2026-06-03 · **Data:** qhfi lake (yfinance adj-close OHLCV + PIT fundamentals)
**Status:** research-only — no framework code committed; numbers generated by a throwaway scratch script (since deleted).

---

## TL;DR

1. **13 candidate alphas** were screened beyond the existing 8 Alpha101 starters + 3 library factors (all of which are price/volume-only).
2. **One genuine, tradable find: `amihud_illiq` (illiquidity premium).** It is the only candidate that is statistically significant *and* survives realistic costs *and* holds up out-of-sample: NET Sharpe **+0.49**, walk-forward OOS Sharpe **+0.37**, orthogonal to the library (max |ρ| = 0.21), slow-decaying. It still **fails the promotion gate** (needs Sharpe ≥ 1.0) and the multiple-testing bar (Deflated Sharpe 40% < 50%), so it is a **watchlist** signal, not a promote.
3. **`mom_12_1` and `quality_momentum`** have significant IC but are **gross-flat and net-negative** — a textbook reminder that a significant IC is *not* a tradable alpha once costs are paid.
4. **Fundamentals are coverage-crippled on this pool** — `earnings_yield`/`value_momentum` are 39-day artifacts; EDGAR-totals signals (gross profitability, accruals, asset growth) cover only 27–62 names and show negligible IC. **Actionable infra finding**, not an alpha finding.
5. **Methodology finding:** the qhfi default backtest config (`$100k` book + `$1` min-ticket commission + integer shares) is a **toy for a ~100-name book** — the per-ticket floor dwarfs the book and manufactures spurious −99% drawdowns. All economic numbers here use a realistic **$50M** fractional-share book.

---

## Method

Mirrors the two existing research scripts (`scripts/eval_alpha101.py`, `scripts/build_multialpha.py`); all evaluation reuses qhfi tooling (`factors.evaluation`, `factors.transforms`, `factors.selection`, `backtest.engine`, `evaluation.deflated_sharpe`). Per candidate:

- **Signal hygiene** — raw panel → `zscore` → sector-`neutralize` (same prep as the starter-set eval). Signs are informational (a negative-IC alpha is just its inverse).
- **Predictive strength** — cross-sectional rank IC at 1/5/21-day horizons, IC-IR (mean/σ), t-stat, hit-rate.
- **Tradability** — quintile spread (Q5−Q1), IC decay across (1,2,3,5,10,21)d, rank autocorrelation (turnover proxy).
- **Orthogonality** (the key "new-alpha" test) — max Spearman |ρ| vs the existing 11 library signals, plus iterative VIF pruning over {price candidates ∪ library}.
- **Costed backtest** — `FactorStrategy` (q=0.2, sector-neutral) → gross (zero-cost) vs net ($50M book, default costs) → walk-forward OOS → `Scorecard`.
- **Multiple-testing** — Deflated Sharpe of the top picks against the full 13-candidate trial set.

---

## 1. Candidate roster

| Candidate | Formula | Dir | Bucket | Anomaly / ref |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `nh52_proximity` | `close / ts_max(close,252)` | +1 | price | 52-week high (George–Hwang) |
| `max_lottery` | `ts_max(returns,21)` | −1 | price | MAX effect (Bali et al.) |
| `amihud_illiq` | `ts_mean(\|ret\|/(close·vol),21)` | +1 | price | Illiquidity premium (Amihud) |
| `volume_shock` | `volume / ts_mean(volume,21)` | −1 | price | Abnormal-volume reversal |
| `ret_skew` | rolling 60d skew of returns | −1 | price | Idiosyncratic-skew preference |
| `mom_12_1` | `close[-21]/close[-252] − 1` | +1 | price | Classic 12-1 momentum |
| `gross_profitability` | `gross_profit / total_assets` | +1 | fund | Novy-Marx |
| `accruals` | `(net_income − ocf) / total_assets` | −1 | fund | Sloan |
| `asset_growth` | report-over-report %Δ total_assets | −1 | fund | Cooper–Gulen–Schill |
| `earnings_yield` | `eps_ttm / close` | +1 | fund | Value (E/P) |
| `leverage` | `debt_to_equity` | −1 | fund | Distress/leverage |
| `quality_momentum` | z(gross_prof) + z(momentum) | +1 | combo | Quality-at-a-trend |
| `value_momentum` | z(earnings_yield) + z(momentum) | +1 | combo | Value + momentum |

---

## 2. IC scorecard (ranked by |IC-IR 5d|)

`avg_n_raw` = avg names/day with a value (coverage). `IC5_raw` = 5d IC **without** sector-neutralization (the meaningful read for thin fundamentals).

| alpha | bucket | avg_n | IC_1d | IC_5d | IC_21d | IC-IR_5d | t_5d | IC5_raw | autocorr | days |
|---|---|--:|--:|--:|--:|--:|--:|--:|--:|--:|
| earnings_yield | fund | **0** | −0.023 | −0.159 | −0.442 | −0.386 | −2.4 | −0.080 | 0.98 | **39** |
| amihud_illiq | price | 99 | 0.004 | **0.0097** | 0.022 | **0.084** | **6.8** | – | 0.996 | 6618 |
| mom_12_1 | price | 96 | 0.011 | **0.0136** | 0.012 | **0.083** | **6.7** | – | 0.992 | 6387 |
| value_momentum | combo | **0** | −0.020 | 0.020 | −0.140 | 0.068 | 0.4 | 0.018 | 0.98 | **39** |
| leverage | fund | **1** | 0.002 | 0.011 | −0.011 | 0.050 | 0.8 | 0.007 | 1.00 | 249 |
| quality_momentum | combo | 27 | 0.007 | 0.008 | 0.008 | 0.046 | 3.0 | 0.012 | 0.99 | 4238 |
| volume_shock | price | 100 | 0.008 | 0.004 | −0.003 | 0.040 | 3.3 | – | 0.43 | 6619 |
| nh52_proximity | price | 96 | 0.001 | −0.005 | −0.010 | −0.028 | −2.2 | – | 0.98 | 6388 |
| gross_profitability | fund | 27 | 0.003 | 0.004 | 0.005 | 0.026 | 1.7 | 0.010 | 1.00 | 4238 |
| max_lottery | price | 100 | −0.004 | 0.002 | 0.010 | 0.016 | 1.3 | – | 0.95 | 6618 |
| accruals | fund | 58 | 0.002 | 0.002 | −0.000 | 0.010 | 0.7 | −0.003 | 1.00 | 4288 |
| asset_growth | fund | 62 | 0.000 | 0.001 | 0.003 | 0.009 | 0.6 | 0.004 | 0.98 | 4226 |
| ret_skew | price | 99 | −0.001 | −0.000 | −0.006 | −0.003 | −0.2 | – | 0.98 | 6579 |

**Baseline (sanity-check).** The Alpha101 starter set on the same pool runs IC-IR 0.02–**0.17** (best: alpha054 0.171, alpha033 0.129) with ICs of 0.004–0.023. The new candidates' magnitudes sit inside that band — no look-ahead inflation — but the **best new signal (IC-IR ≈ 0.084) is only about half as strong as the best existing starter.** The candidates earn their keep on *orthogonality*, not raw strength.

**Reliability flags.** `earnings_yield`/`value_momentum` (eps_ttm, 39 valid days, avg_n≈0 after neutralization) and `leverage` (249 days) are **small-sample artifacts** — their large negative numbers are noise, not signal. They are screened out of all downstream backtests.

---

## 3. Orthogonality — are these genuinely *new*?

Max Spearman |ρ| of each candidate's sector-neutral signal vs the 11 existing library signals:

| candidate | closest library factor | max \|ρ\| | verdict |
|---|---|--:|---|
| volume_shock | alpha004 | **0.07** | highly orthogonal |
| amihud_illiq | volatility | **0.21** | orthogonal ✔ |
| ret_skew | momentum | 0.23 | orthogonal |
| mom_12_1 | momentum | 0.47 | partly redundant w/ library momentum |
| nh52_proximity | momentum | 0.55 | overlaps momentum |
| value_momentum | volatility | 0.52 | (unreliable) |
| max_lottery | volatility | 0.62 | overlaps low-vol |
| quality_momentum | momentum | 0.66 | mostly its momentum leg |

**VIF prune** over {6 price candidates ∪ 11 library}, threshold 5.0 → **all 6 price candidates survive** (none dropped). At the pooled level, every price candidate carries information the current library does not span — the library is *broad but not saturated*. `amihud_illiq` and `volume_shock` are the most differentiated.

---

## 4. IC decay (mean IC by horizon) — survivors

| alpha | 1d | 2d | 3d | 5d | 10d | 21d | shape |
|---|--:|--:|--:|--:|--:|--:|---|
| **amihud_illiq** | 0.004 | 0.005 | 0.006 | 0.010 | 0.016 | **0.022** | *rises* with horizon → slow, low-turnover edge ✔ |
| mom_12_1 | 0.011 | 0.014 | 0.014 | 0.014 | 0.013 | 0.012 | flat → persistent |
| quality_momentum | 0.007 | 0.008 | 0.008 | 0.008 | 0.011 | 0.008 | flat |
| volume_shock | 0.008 | 0.007 | 0.007 | 0.004 | 0.001 | −0.003 | fast decay → short-lived |
| nh52_proximity | 0.001 | −0.001 | −0.002 | −0.005 | −0.007 | −0.010 | sign-flips → unstable |

`amihud_illiq`'s IC **grows** with horizon — the illiquidity premium accrues over weeks, ideal for a low-turnover book. `volume_shock`'s edge is gone by 10 days (intraday/very-short only).

---

## 5. Multiple-testing (Deflated Sharpe)

Analytic daily long/short returns of the top-3 reliable candidates, deflated against the full 13-candidate search:

| candidate | daily SR | ann SR | DSR = P(true SR > 0 \| 13 trials) |
|---|--:|--:|--:|
| **amihud_illiq** | +0.047 | +0.75 | **40.0%** |
| quality_momentum | +0.011 | +0.17 | 0.5% |
| mom_12_1 | +0.007 | +0.11 | 0.0% |

Even the best candidate clears only 40% — i.e. **no candidate convincingly beats the "luckiest of 13 draws" threshold.** Honest read: these are weak edges; `amihud_illiq` is the only one in the neighbourhood of significance after the multiplicity penalty.

---

## 6. Costed mini-backtest — gross vs net

`FactorStrategy` q=0.2, sector-neutral, **$50M fractional-share book**. Gross = zero cost; Net = per-share 0.5¢ commission + 5bps slippage + 50/100bps financing. Walk-forward = 48 folds (504d train / 126d test / 10d purge).

| candidate | annTO | GROSS Sharpe / CAGR / maxDD | NET Sharpe / CAGR / maxDD | OOS Sharpe | cost drag | scorecard |
|---|--:|---|---|--:|--:|---|
| **amihud_illiq** | 24.6× | **+0.94** / +5.4% / −13.0% | **+0.49** / +2.7% / −15.4% | **+0.37** | +2.7%/yr | FAIL (Sharpe<1) |
| mom_12_1 | 31.5× | +0.10 / +0.5% / −33.4% | −0.35 / −2.8% / −66.3% | −0.40 | +3.3%/yr | FAIL |
| quality_momentum | 23.6× | +0.18 / +0.9% / −15.0% | −0.21 / −1.3% / −32.4% | −0.21 | +2.2%/yr | FAIL |

- **`amihud_illiq` is the only candidate positive net of costs *and* out-of-sample** (NET +0.49, OOS +0.37, OOS-robustness check passes). It fails promotion only on the absolute Sharpe≥1.0 bar.
- **`mom_12_1`** is gross-flat (+0.10) and net-negative — its IC significance does not convert to P&L on this pool, and it largely duplicates library momentum anyway.
- All three carry **20–30× annual turnover** even though their signals are very stable (autocorr ≈0.99). That turnover is inflated by quintile-boundary churn + rebalancing; a no-trade band / signal smoothing (both exist in `portfolio/construction.py`) is the obvious lever to recover much of the cost drag — untested here.

### Methodology note — the toy-config trap
With the qhfi **default** ($100k book, $1 min-ticket commission, integer shares), the same `amihud_illiq` backtest reports CAGR −5% and maxDD −78.6%, and `mom_12_1` reports **maxDD −99.7%**. That is an artifact: a ~100-name dollar-neutral book on $100k is ~$1.25k/position, so integer-share rounding churns most names daily and each tiny trade hits the **$1 commission floor** — commissions reach **0.9× the entire book**. Always size the book so the per-ticket floor is negligible (or set `allow_fractional: true`) before reading equity-strategy backtests.

---

## 7. Recommendations

| Signal | Verdict | Next step |
|---|---|---|
| **`amihud_illiq`** | **Watchlist — promote candidate.** Orthogonal, slow-decaying, positive net & OOS; only the absolute-Sharpe bar fails. | Add a no-trade band / smoothing to cut the 24× turnover; re-test at lower rebalance frequency; consider blending into the library as a new `IlliquidityFactor`. Verify it isn't just loading on the smallest large-caps (size confound). |
| `volume_shock` | Niche — very orthogonal but fast-decaying (dead by 10d). | Only viable intraday/T+1; out of scope for a daily book. |
| `mom_12_1`, `nh52_proximity`, `max_lottery` | Redundant with library momentum/low-vol (ρ 0.47–0.62); net-negative or weak. | Drop. |
| `ret_skew` | No edge here (IC ≈ 0). | Drop. |
| Fundamentals (all) | **Not evaluable on this pool** — coverage 0–62 names, sector-neutralization collapses the cross-section. | **Infra task, not research:** backfill the EDGAR/yfinance fundamentals lake to full `equity_sectors` coverage (esp. `eps_ttm`, `roe`, `gross_margin` at ~30 names) before any fundamental alpha can be judged. Re-run this study on `sp500` (deeper cross-section) once covered. |

**Bottom line:** the price/volume anomaly space on this 100-name pool is largely already spanned by the existing library; the one orthogonal, cost-surviving addition is the **illiquidity premium**, and even it needs turnover control to clear the promotion gate. The bigger leverage is **data coverage** — the fundamentals lake is too sparse here to test the (historically strongest) fundamental anomalies.

---

## 8. Caveats & reproduction

- **Prices** are yfinance adjusted-close-only → intraday open/high/low are adjusted approximations; true VWAP unavailable (same caveat the starter-set scripts print).
- **No survivorship-free universe-as-of-date** — `equity_sectors` is a current-membership pool; small survivorship tilt in long-horizon stats.
- **PIT integrity** maintained: fundamentals are report-date-stamped + forward-filled; `asset_growth` is computed on each name's sparse report series *before* the daily ffill (no look-ahead).
- **Reproduce:** the analysis was generated by a one-off scratch script (`scripts/_scratch_new_alpha.py`, removed after this report). It reused `MarketPanels`, `FundamentalsStore`, `factors.{operators,transforms,evaluation,selection}`, `FactorStrategy`, `BacktestEngine`, `walk_forward`, and `deflated_sharpe` — to rebuild it, point a copy of `eval_alpha101.py` at the candidate roster in §1 and add the gross/net backtest from §6 (zero-cost engine + $50M fractional book).
